Title: Rice plastidial NAD-dependent malate dehydrogenase 1 negatively regulates salt stress response by reducing the vitamin B6 content

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPLR1 transcript level was substantially reduced in OsMDH1 overexpression lines. 2. The paper proposes that reduced OsPLR1 expression may contribute to lower pyridoxine accumulation under salt stress. 3. OsPLR1 is treated as a key vitamin B6 pathway gene affected downstream of OsMDH1, but it was not directly functionally manipulated in this study.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lower expression is associated with reduced pyridoxine content in OsMDH1 overexpression plants.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant/cofactor contributing to ROS detoxification and salt tolerance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a key downstream metabolism gene explicitly discussed as contributing to pyridoxine reduction, but evidence is correlative only in this paper.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress context; measured mainly under normal-condition transcriptomics in OsMDH1OX vs NIP

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPDX1.1 expression was strongly reduced in OsMDH1 overexpression plants. 2. The paper infers that this reduction may contribute to decreased vitamin B6/pyridoxine levels. 3. This gene is discussed as a key enzyme gene in vitamin B6 biosynthesis downstream of OsMDH1.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression is associated with lower pyridoxine accumulation in OsMDH1 overexpression plants.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin B6 cofactor/antioxidant involved in ROS detoxification and salt tolerance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally validated in this study; downstream transcript change only.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress relevance inferred from overall study

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPDX1.2 expression was substantially reduced in OsMDH1 overexpression plants. 2. The authors infer that this contributes to reduced pyridoxine/vitamin B6 accumulation. 3. It is one of the key vitamin B6 biosynthesis genes altered by OsMDH1.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression is associated with lower pyridoxine content in OsMDH1 overexpression plants.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin B6 cofactor/antioxidant involved in ROS detoxification and salt tolerance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream association only; not directly manipulated.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress relevance inferred from overall study

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPDX2 expression was significantly reduced in OsMDH1 overexpression plants. 2. The study proposes that this reduction may affect vitamin B6 homeostasis and pyridoxine content. 3. OsPDX2 is highlighted as a key enzyme gene in vitamin B6 biosynthesis altered downstream of OsMDH1.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression is associated with lower pyridoxine/vitamin B6 content in OsMDH1 overexpression plants.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin B6 cofactor/antioxidant involved in ROS detoxification and salt tolerance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly manipulated; evidence is transcript-level association in OsMDH1OX background.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress relevance inferred from overall study

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss-of-function osmdh1 mutants are more salt tolerant, while OsMDH1 overexpression lines are salt sensitive. 2. OsMDH1 encodes a plastid-localized NAD-dependent malate dehydrogenase that increases malate accumulation. 3. OsMDH1 negatively regulates salt-induced pyridoxine (vitamin B6) accumulation, thereby increasing ROS and reducing salt tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; complementation with OsMDH1pro:OsMDH1-GFP; overexpression; chloroplast localization by GFP transient expression; in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant GST-OsMDH1; in vivo chloroplast NAD-MDH activity assay; LC-MS metabolite profiling; HPLC pyridoxine quantification; salt stress phenotyping; pyridoxine rescue assay; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression caused early flowering and salt hypersensitivity with increased ROS; loss-of-function caused late flowering.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Malate dehydrogenase family; plastidial NAD-dependent MDH; contains conserved NAD+-binding and proton acceptor/catalytic histidine site; chloroplast transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin B6 vitamer functioning as an antioxidant/cofactor; contributes to ROS detoxification and salt stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces pyridoxine accumulation and thereby negatively affects salt stress tolerance; increases malate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The metabolite effect on pyridoxine is indirect: OsMDH1 alters expression of vitamin B6 biosynthetic/salvage genes rather than directly catalyzing vitamin B6 synthesis. Salt stress rapidly induces OsMDH1 expression and plastidial NAD-MDH activity.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression from pCsV1300 construct promoter not explicitly stated; complementation under native OsMDH1 promoter; CRISPR/Cas9 used Pubi/UBQ10-related expression system for Cas9 in construct generation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 mM NaCl salt stress; exogenous 10 μM pyridoxine rescue treatment
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly reduces oxaloacetate to malate using NADH in the plastid/chloroplast.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream/central carbon metabolism step supplying malate rather than a vitamin B6 pathway step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Indirectly modulates target product by altering expression of vitamin B6 biosynthetic/salvage genes and cellular metabolic state
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Malate metabolism; indirect regulator of vitamin B6 metabolism/homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaf, leaf sheath, panicle, glume, bud, root and stem; rapidly induced by NaCl
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established for malate pathway; supported as a key control point for salt-stress-associated pyridoxine homeostasis in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function/editing of OsMDH1 could be used to improve salt tolerance and elevate pyridoxine under salinity, but flowering-time effects should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed broadly in vegetative and reproductive tissues; induced within hours by salt treatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA528686; LC-MS metabolomics (accession NA)
